title: Dynamic Island compact view renders too wide
description: In compact Dynamic Island presentation, the timer icon and countdown are rendering as a full-width bar instead of staying tight to the camera cutout pill. Compact leading/trailing views need to be constrained to small sizes (caption2 font, minimal padding). Fix in TimersWidgetExtensionLiveActivity.swift compactLeading and compactTrailing closures.

title: Lock screen Live Activity should show 'Group : Name' not just name
description: LockScreenView in TimersWidgetExtensionLiveActivity.swift uses context.attributes.profileName which is just the timer name. When a timer belongs to a group, the display should be 'Group : Name' (e.g. 'Tea : Black'). Requires adding groupName: String? to TimerAttributes, threading group through TimerInstance, and updating LockScreenView.

title: Duration picker shows only hours and minutes, missing seconds
description: UIDatePicker in countDownTimer mode is only showing hours and minutes dials — seconds dial is absent. Expected behavior matches the Clock app which shows all three (h/m/s). Root cause likely requires investigation of UIDatePicker minuteInterval or a known iOS bug where countDownTimer mode drops seconds.
close_reason: Replaced UIDatePicker (h/m only) with custom UIPickerView showing hours, minutes, and seconds columns.

title: Duration picker shows only hours and minutes, missing seconds
description: UIDatePicker in countDownTimer mode is only showing hours and minutes dials — seconds dial is absent. Expected behavior matches the Clock app which shows all three (h/m/s). Root cause likely requires investigation of UIDatePicker minuteInterval or a known iOS bug where countDownTimer mode drops seconds.

title: Live Activity UI polish: larger countdown, delete button
description:
Improve the lock screen and expanded Dynamic Island views to match the Clock app's richer presentation.

Changes:
- Lock screen countdown: increase to title/largeTitle font size
- Add a delete/cancel button on the lock screen view so the user can stop the timer without unlocking the phone (use Link with a custom URL scheme or ActivityKit button API)
- Expanded Dynamic Island bottom region: show cancel button alongside the progress arc
- Timer name/group label styling improvements as needed

title: Live Activity: pause button in lock screen and expanded Dynamic Island
description:
Add a pause/resume button to the Live Activity views, using the pause/resume engine support from timers-6zl.

Changes:
- Lock screen view: add pause/resume toggle button next to the cancel button
- Expanded Dynamic Island bottom region: pause/resume button
- TimerAttributes.ContentState.isPaused drives button state and countdown display (frozen when paused)
- Compact trailing: show pause indicator (e.g. different icon) when paused

title: Pause/resume support for timer instances
description:
Add pause and resume to TimerEngine. When paused, the countdown freezes and the notification/Live Activity should reflect the paused state.

Engine changes needed:
- Add .paused case to InstanceState
- Add pausedAt: Date? to TimerInstance (set when paused, cleared on resume)
- TimerEngine.pause(_ instance:): sets state to .paused, records pausedAt, cancels pending notification, updates Live Activity
- TimerEngine.resume(_ instance:): recalculates startTime based on elapsed time before pause, resets state to .running, reschedules notification, updates Live Activity
- tick() should skip .paused instances
- handleBackground/handleForeground: persist and restore paused state correctly (paused instances should not be expired on foreground)
- TimerAttributes.ContentState: add isPaused: Bool so Live Activity can show paused state
- Unit tests for pause, resume, pause-then-background-then-foreground

title: Live Activity UI: larger countdown, pause and delete buttons
description:
The current lock screen Live Activity is minimal (just name + countdown). Goal is to match the Clock app's richer presentation: larger countdown font, and action buttons for pause and delete directly on the lock screen / expanded Dynamic Island.

Scope:
- Lock screen view: make the countdown font larger (title or largeTitle size)
- Add a delete/cancel button so the user can stop the timer from the lock screen without unlocking
- Add a pause button (note: pause is not currently implemented in TimerEngine — this may require a new .paused InstanceState, a pausedAt: Date field, and corresponding TimerEngine.pause/resume methods, plus ContentState changes in TimerAttributes)
- Expanded Dynamic Island view should also show these buttons

Note: pause support is a significant engine change. May want to split into two issues (UI polish vs pause feature) during implementation.
close_reason: Split into separate tickets for UI polish and pause button

title: Sound picker: enumerate all available notification tones
description:
Currently SoundPickerView only shows a single 'Default' option (AvailableSounds.all in SoundPickerView.swift). Goal is to surface all tones the user can choose from — ideally matching what the Clock app offers.

Investigation needed: iOS does not expose a public API to enumerate device-installed ringtones or custom tones for use with UNNotificationSound. Options to explore:
1. System notification sounds bundled with iOS — accessible as named .caf files at known paths (e.g. /System/Library/Audio/UISounds/) but reading that directory from a sandboxed app may be restricted
2. Bundle a curated set of .caf sound files in the app target (reliable, no API restrictions, but manual curation)
3. Check if AVFoundation or any public framework exposes tone enumeration

Expected outcome: SoundPickerView shows a meaningful list of named tones. If device-wide tone enumeration is not possible via public APIs, bundle a good default set (e.g. the standard iOS notification sounds as .caf files) and document the limitation.

title: Countdown font uses monospaced serif (typewriter) instead of monospaced digits
description: The remaining-time countdown in TimerInstanceRow uses .system(.body, design: .monospaced) which renders as a typewriter/serif monospaced font. Should use .monospacedDigit() on the default system font, or .fontDesign(.monospaced) with a round/modern feel — not the courier-style serif. Fix in TimerInstanceRow.swift CountdownText subview.
close_reason: Removed design: .monospaced from font modifier; .monospacedDigit() alone gives fixed-width digits in the standard SF font.

title: UI design pass: visual polish and layout refinement
description:
The current UI is functional but bland — default system styling throughout, no visual personality, and layout that hasn't been carefully considered. This is a design-and-implementation task that will need exploration before a specific approach can be specified.

Areas to consider:
- Timer list rows: visual weight, spacing, hierarchy between name/duration/group
- Active instance rows: the 'running' state should feel alive (color, animation, countdown prominence)
- Finished state: the green glow is a start but could be more intentional
- Section headers and group separators
- Navigation bar: title treatment, button sizing/placement
- Ad hoc and profile editor sheets: form styling, picker presentation
- Color usage: currently monochrome; consider a considered accent color strategy
- Typography: system defaults throughout; consider size/weight hierarchy
- Empty state: no timers defined yet — needs a prompt or illustration

Approach: recommend a focused design exploration (possibly with visual mockups) before implementation, rather than incremental changes file by file. The goal is a coherent visual language, not a collection of local improvements.

title: App has no icon
description: App icon is the default placeholder. Need to add an actual app icon asset to Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset.

title: App has no icon
description: App icon is the default placeholder. Need to add an actual app icon asset to Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset.
